channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 🚀 CrowdBooks is LIVE — and it's getting better every day!
item.description: A few days ago I shared how I built a collaborative book platform with no admins and no gatekeepers. Today I'm happy to report: we're live, and the latest update just dropped some features I'm...

item.title: US bans new foreign-made consumer Internet routers
item.description: Article URL: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c74787w149zo Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47498108 Points: 4 # Comments: 1

item.title: Why REST-Based Video Uploads Failed — and Why We Moved to Azure Communication Services
item.description: At AIHYR, an AI-driven interview platform, we focus on building reliable and scalable interview experiences where every candidate interaction can be accurately evaluated. In such systems,...

item.title: Why my Next.js dashboard was always showing stale data — and the one-line fix
item.description: The timestamp was updating. The AI recommendations kept coming in. Everything looked alive. But the sensor values hadn't moved in twenty minutes. That's the kind of bug that gets inside your head....

item.title: CVE-2026-33169: CVE-2026-33169: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in ActiveSupport Number Formatting
item.description: CVE-2026-33169: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in ActiveSupport Number Formatting Vulnerability ID: CVE-2026-33169 CVSS Score: 6.9 Published: 2026-03-23 CVE-2026-33169 is a Regular...

item.title: LaTeX for Academic Publishing: What Journals Actually Require (And What's Optional)
item.description: A practical guide based on real journal submission experience - not the textbook version. "Submit in LaTeX" covers a lot of ground. Some journals require it strictly. Others accept Word but...

item.title: Stop Writing Structs by Hand: Generate Go, Java, TypeScript, and 8 More from JSON
item.description: Automatic Code Generation from JSON JSON Buddy's Code Generator converts any JSON object into a ready-to-use struct or class definition. Paste JSON, pick a language, copy the output. Done in...

item.title: Your AI Can Submit Apps to the App Store Now. No, Seriously.
item.description: I spent 45 minutes uploading screenshots to App Store Connect last Tuesday. Different sizes for every device. 6.7-inch, 6.5-inch, 5.5-inch — because apparently Apple thinks we still care about the...

item.title: Why Purging Nginx Cache Is Only Half the Job (And How I Built the Other Half)
item.description: If you're self-hosting WordPress behind Nginx with caching, you've probably relied on plugins to automatically purge your cache. 📝 Post updated → 🗑 cache wiped → ✅ done. Except it's not done. The...

item.title: How to Build a React Native MRZ Passport Scanner for Android and iOS
item.description: Verifying travel documents manually is error-prone and slow. The Machine Readable Zone (MRZ) printed on passports, ID cards, and other ICAO-compliant travel documents encodes all key identity...

item.title: A New Framework for Evaluation of Voice Agents (EVA)
The US government just banned consumer routers made outside the US
item.description: In December, the Federal Communications Commission banned all future drones made in foreign countries from being imported into the United States, unless or until their maker gets an exemption....

item.title: Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
item.description: London’s Air Street Capital has raised a large Fund III with eyes locked on backing early-stage European and North American AI companies.

item.title: The kid-friendly Fitbit Ace is $100, which matches its best price
item.description: If you’re trying to figure out how to keep your kids occupied this spring break, the Fitbit Ace LTE is an easy way to get them outdoors and off their phones. The kid-friendly smartwatch encourages...

item.title: Kalshi says it will block politicians and athletes from trading in markets they’re tied to
item.description: The prediction market platform Kalshi says it's rolling out new guardrails to "preemptively block" political candidates and athletes from trading in their own markets, as reported earlier by...

item.title: Bernie Sanders’ AI ‘gotcha’ video flops, but the memes are great
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders thinks he's tricked Claude into revealing the AI industry's secrets, but he really just exposed how agreeable chatbots can become.

item.title: Google’s new Pixel 10 ads made me go ‘Wait, WHAT are they trying to sell?’
item.description: Ever watch a TV ad and wonder, "How did this get approved?" Today, Google has not one but two new ad spots for its six-month-old Pixel 10 phones, and… let's just say they may not come across as...

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says ‘I think we’ve achieved AGI’
item.description: On a Monday episode of the Lex Fridman podcast, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang made a hot-button statement: "I think we've achieved AGI." AGI, or artificial general intelligence, is a vaguely defined...

item.title: Two of my favorite color e-book readers are the cheapest they’ve been in months
item.description: Color isn’t essential in an e-reader, but let’s be honest, it’s a nice perk that can bring digital books, magazines, comics, cookbooks, and other publications to life. The catch is that color...

item.title: Apple could put ads in Maps as soon as this summer
item.description: Apple will soon bring advertisements to its Maps app on mobile and the web, according to a report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. The ads, which Apple could reportedly announce this month before...

item.title: Vibe-coding startup Lovable is on the hunt for acquisitions
item.description: Lovable's founder said the fast-growing vibe-coding startup is looking for startups and teams to join its company.

item.title: North Korean Hackers Abuse VS Code Auto-Run Tasks to Deploy StoatWaffle Malware
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the Contagious Interview campaign, also tracked as WaterPlum, have been attributed to a malware family tracked as StoatWaffle that's distributed via malicious...

item.title: Apple sets June date for WWDC 2026, teasing ‘AI advancements’
item.description: Apple will host its next Worldwide Developers Conference the week of June 8. The company is expected to announce major updates to Siri with advanced AI capabilities.

item.title: Donut Lab’s solid-state battery could barely hold a charge after getting damaged
item.description: Donut Lab is on a mission to prove to the world its solid-state battery is real, one independent test at a time. We've seen speed charging tests, extreme heat tests, and not-a-supercapacitor...

item.title: Apple’s WWDC 2026 event starts June 8th
item.description: Apple has set a date for this year's Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC), scheduling the keynote and Platforms State of the Union for June 8th through June 12th at Apple Park. As usual, we will...

item.title: PDP’s wireless guitar controller has returned to its best price to date
item.description: The early 2020s were kind of a dark time if you were a fan of Rock Band. PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X owners didn’t get an improved, more feature-packed guitar controller until early 2024 when...

item.title: The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. I was originally going to write this week’s newsletter...

item.title: Startup Gimlet Labs is solving the AI inference bottleneck in a surprisingly elegant way
item.description: Gimlet Labs just raised an $80 million Series A for tech that lets AI run across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras and d-Matrix chips, simultaneously.

item.title: Littlebird raises $11M for its AI-assisted ‘recall’ tool that reads your computer screen
item.description: Littlebird is building an AI that reads your screen in real time to capture context, answer questions, and automate tasks, without relying on screenshots.

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on...
